issuesFound.title: missing page title

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: This button toggles the main navigation menu but lacks an aria-expanded attribute. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 38: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides an outline-offset. Without a non-zero outline, box-shadow, or border, there is no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the outline for elements that do not match :focus-visible. If a browser fails to support :focus-visible or incorrectly categorizes an interaction, the focus indicator is lost entirely.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the focus indicator is set to outline: none. In high-contrast mode, box-shadows are often disabled, making a visible outline mandatory for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 327: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a replacement (like a box-shadow or border) within the same rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline. Keyboard users will not be able to visually track their position when navigating to the site home link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus indicator. This prevents keyboard users on mobile-width viewports from identifying the active element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the outline and only provide an outline-offset. Since the outline width is zero by default (due to the global reset), no indicator appears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on focus. While it provides a box-shadow, box-shadows are often stripped in High Contrast Mode, leaving the element without an indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input explicitly removes the outline for focus-visible states, which are specifically intended for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submission button removes the focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to verify they have reached the submit action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 748: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline, failing to provide a visible indicator for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" links in feature cards remove the focus outline, which is a failure of SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus indicator, preventing keyboard users from knowing which category filter they are about to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle buttons remove the focus outline, creating a barrier for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1301: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table container is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable region is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1434: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus outline, making it difficult for keyboard users to navigate the list of questions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls remove the outline on focus. While a box-shadow is provided, it is insufficient for users in High Contrast Mode where shadows are removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1638: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls explicitly remove the focus-visible outline, which is the primary indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1639: .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 561: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or those using touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: min-width: 24px; Line 575: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class has a min-height of 32px. While it meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px AAA recommendation and may be difficult to use without sufficient spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 488: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu toggle is sized at 28px, which is significantly smaller than the recommended 44px target for critical navigation elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 606: min-height: 44px; Line 607: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 658: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links are sized at 28px height, which may lead to accidental activations on touch devices due to the small target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 682: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size for form inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 704: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px by 28px, which is a small target for a primary search action.
issuesFound.fix: Line 734: width: 44px; Line 735: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the recommended 44px target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1283: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .product-body .btn-secondary
issuesFound.explanation: Product CTA buttons are restricted to a 28px min-height, making them harder to tap on mobile devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1211: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class resets width/height to auto and overflow to visible. This fails to visually hide the content if the element contains text, and the lack of clipping means it may interfere with the visual layout.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: width: 1px; Line 347: height: 1px; Line 350: overflow: hidden; Line 351: clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); clip-path: inset(50%); position: absolute;

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting word-spacing to -0.2em crowds text together, making it difficult for users with low vision or dyslexia to distinguish individual words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Negative letter-spacing (-0.05em) reduces the legibility of body text by causing characters to overlap or appear too close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em), which significantly degrades readability for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but there is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes a transform transition. Users who are sensitive to motion may experience vestibular distress when buttons move on hover/active states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The arrow icon in feature card links translates horizontally on hover. This spatial movement lacks a reduced motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1000: .feature-card a::after { content: "→"; transition: none; display: inline-block; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion arrow rotates via a transition. While a media query exists on line 1588, it only targets the transition property, not the transform itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1588: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global reset removes underlines from all links. In body text (like .prose or paragraphs), this makes links indistinguishable from text for colorblind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.32:1
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary text color (--colour-text-secondary) used against the page background (--colour-bg-page) fails the minimum contrast requirement of 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 74: --colour-text-secondary: #656565;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #ffffff ratio 2.54:1
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text color (--colour-neutral-400) used in inputs against a white background fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio for informative text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 23: --colour-neutral-400: #6b7280;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.18:1
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker (.required-marker) uses --colour-error (#ee9999) against a white background, making the "required" signal unreadable for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 38: --colour-error: #d32f2f;

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `nav-open` class on the body and the `is-open` class on the drawer, but they never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu has been deployed or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ...`
  Line 1561: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ...`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTabClick` function in the product filter controller updates the active category but does not update the `aria-pressed` state of the filter buttons. Since these buttons act as toggles for the current view, their state must be programmatically available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: `function _onTabClick(e) { ... _productFilterState.activeCategory = value; qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === value))); ...`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` adds an error class and updates an error message element, but it does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field itself. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state when the user navigates to it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: `function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { if (result.valid) { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... } else { field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... } }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger button. This leaves the initial state of the widget ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1618: `function init() { ... _initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller uses the `hidden` attribute to hide panels, but the CSS transition logic in `_closeInstance` delays the application of the attribute. During the transition, the element is visually fading but remains fully available in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1665: `function _openInstance(inst) { ... inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); ... }`
  Line 1678: `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user changes the sort order via the `_onSortChange` handler, the product grid is re-ordered and updated, but no announcement is made to screen readers that the sort has been applied or that the list order has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2265: `function _onSortChange(e) { ... applyFilters(); liveRegion.announce('Products sorted by ' + parts[0]); }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a `click` listener to the `zoneEl` (a `div`) to trigger the file input, but it does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key. Only the `Enter` key is handled, which violates the expectation that custom buttons respond to both keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3585: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form contains a "Full name" input field (`name="full_name"`) but does not include an `autocomplete` attribute. WCAG 2.1 requires that inputs collecting personal data have an appropriate autocomplete value to assist users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3285: `const nameField = qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl); if (nameField) nameField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion triggers are linked to their panels via `aria-controls` inside the `faqRegistry.parse` function, but if the panel does not have an ID, the reference is broken or missing. The code ensures an ID exists but does not verify the trigger's `aria-controls` attribute is set if it was missing from the source HTML.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755: `if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', panelId);`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but fail to update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1445: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1461: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds a visual error class and inserts an error message but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state.

issuesFound.fix: Line 3180: `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: `table.setAttribute('hidden', ''); table.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module turns a `<div>` into a drop zone with `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`, but it only handles `keydown` for Enter and Space. It does not ensure that the underlying file input is accessible if the JS fails or if the user expects standard file input behavior.

issuesFound.title: missing programmatic focus management on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `cookieConsent` banner is a modal dialog (`role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`) but the code uses a `setTimeout` to focus the first button. If the timeout is too short or the element isn't ready, focus remains on the background, violating focus management requirements.

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses properties that do not hide content from screen readers (position: static, overflow: visible, clip: auto). This fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 290: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition on hover but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation, which can cause discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 638: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button has a transform transition on :active but no reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this movement, which can cause discomfort.

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle(), .open(), and .close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2460: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update aria-expanded on their trigger buttons. Screen reader users will not know if dropdowns are open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2530: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2540: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands an FAQ answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified the content is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4020: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates visible product count but does not announce the result via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: inside applyFilters, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to false on page load when the drawer is closed. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: in mobileNav.init(), after `_trigger` is assigned, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2620: in dropdownMenu.register(), after setting aria-haspopup, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer but does not set aria-hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when hidden, potentially causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2460: in mobileNav.close(), add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance() function hides the panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel remains in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2540: in _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close() function collapses the answer panel but does not set aria-hidden="true". The panel remains in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4040: in faqAccordion.close(), add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after setting aria-expanded to false.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function changes billing period and updates prices but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the billing period switch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3480: in setPeriod, after `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation function missing aria-invalid update
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields as invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen reader users are not alerted to the validation error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4300: in _renderResult, inside the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after adding the invalid class.

issuesFound.title: Form validation reset missing aria-invalid clear
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields as valid but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen reader users may continue to hear an invalid state after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4300: in _renderResult, inside the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after adding the valid class.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup but does not set aria-controls pointing to the panel id, even though the panel has an id. Screen reader users cannot programmatically navigate to the controlled panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2620: in register, after setting aria-haspopup, ensure `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);` is called (panelId is already set via srUtils.ensureId).

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in testimonials carousel not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.goTo() function changes the visible slide but does not announce the slide change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the carousel advances.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4700: in goTo, after updating the active slide, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Slide ${target + 1} of ${_slides.length}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch (_toggleEl) is a div with role="switch" and has a click handler but no keydown handler for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the switch using standard keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3480: add an event listener for keydown on _toggleEl that checks for Enter or Space and calls _onToggleClick.

issuesFound.title: Auto-play carousel without user-controllable pause
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel automatically advances slides via setTimeout without a user-controllable pause mechanism that persists across page loads. Users who need more time cannot stop the auto-play permanently.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4700: in play(), check a user preference stored in localStorage (e.g., 'carousel_autoplay') before starting the timer. Provide a button to save the preference.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic filter results in FAQ search not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion._filterByQuery() function shows/hides FAQ items based on search but does not announce the result count via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed how many items match.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4100: in _filterByQuery, after updating visibility, add `liveRegion.announce(count === 0 ? STRINGS.faqNoResults : \`${count} questions found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update in product filter not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: in applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add `liveRegion.announce(\`${totalMatches} products found\`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open(), close(), and toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1548: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1558: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 1564: modify toggle() to call setAttr based on new state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for interactive controls that show/hide content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1666: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1679: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-pressed/aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The _onToggleClick() function toggles billing period but does not update aria-pressed (for button) or aria-checked (for switch) on the toggle element. Users cannot perceive the toggle state via assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3189: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year');` after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions animate the FAQ answer panel but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 for expandable content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3498: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `item.open = true;`. Line 3515: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `item.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields invalid with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3804: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` inside the invalid block. Line 3817: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` inside the valid block.

issuesFound.title: dynamic product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates visible product cards and count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the updated results.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2442: after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates all plan card prices but does not announce the billing period change via a live region. Screen reader users may not perceive the price updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3176: after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: faq accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions expand/collapse FAQ answers but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not know the content has appeared/disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3498: after `item.open = true;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : \`Expanded: ${item.question}\`);`. Line 3515: after `item.open = false;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) : \`Collapsed: ${item.question}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions show/hide the mobile navigation drawer but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not know the menu is now available or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1548: after `_open = true;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. Line 1558: after `_open = false;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide dropdown menus but do not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users may not know a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1666: after `inst.open = true;`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Menu opened: ${inst.trigger.textContent}\`);`. Line 1679: after `inst.open = false;`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Menu closed: ${inst.trigger.textContent}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on mobileNav trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the nav toggle button when the drawer is closed on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1624: after `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on dropdownMenu triggers at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when panels are hidden on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: after `_initialised = true;`, add `_instances.forEach(inst => setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer by removing a CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. The drawer may remain in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1558: after `_open = false;`, add `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`. In open(): `if (_drawer) setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides dropdown panels by adding the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true". While hidden attribute may suffice, aria-hidden ensures removal from AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1679: after `inst.open = false;`, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`. In _openInstance(): `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`.

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive element (theme switcher) missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.init() attaches a click handler to elements with [data-theme-toggle] but does not handle Enter or Space key events. Keyboard users cannot activate the theme toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4649: inside the click event listener, add a keydown handler: `btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); /* same logic as click */ } });`.

issuesFound.title: auto-playing carousel without user-controllable pause/stop
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init() automatically starts playing via play() without a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism that is always visible. This may violate WCAG 2.2.2 for moving content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3638: ensure the play/pause button is always visible and keyboard-focusable, and do not auto-play if prefersReducedMotion() is true. Add `if (prefersReducedMotion()) pause();` before the auto-play check.

issuesFound.title: form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function removes the invalid class but does not set aria-invalid="false" when a field becomes valid. This may leave an incorrect invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3817: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` inside the valid block.

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls (mobileNav)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() does not set aria-controls on the nav toggle button pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users may not understand which element is controlled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1624: after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`.

issuesFound.title: toggle trigger missing aria-controls (dropdownMenu)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-controls but only if the trigger doesn't have it. However, init() does not ensure all triggers have aria-controls. Some dropdown triggers may lack the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: in the register function, ensure `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);` is always called (line 1724).

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button is used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer, but the code never sets or removes `aria‑expanded` on this button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown `<button>` elements receive keyboard and click interaction, but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the expanded state hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not set aria‑expanded when the suggestion panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: When the autocomplete panel becomes visible, the search `<input>` receives no `aria‑expanded` state, so screen‑reader users cannot tell that a list of suggestions is available.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle for billing period updates visual classes but never updates `aria‑pressed` or `aria‑checked`, so its state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling the visual state, call `setAttr(_toggleTrack, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear))` or set `aria-pressed` on the button itself.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing role and tabindex when not a <button> element
issuesFound.explanation: If the `.nav-toggle` element is not a native `<button>`, it lacks `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"`, preventing keyboard focus and activation.
issuesFound.fix: Detect non‑button triggers and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'role', 'button')` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'tabindex', '0')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels lack role or aria‑label when not explicitly provided
issuesFound.explanation: The panel element may be a generic `<ul>` or `<div>` without a semantic role, making its purpose unclear to AT.
issuesFound.fix: If the panel does not already have a role, set `setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu')` or `'region'` and provide an `aria‑label` or `aria‑labelledby` reference.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded updates on programmatic open/close via hash navigation
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened via URL hash, the script calls `open(item)` which sets `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, but the corresponding `aria‑controls` attribute may be missing if the markup omitted it.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each trigger has `aria-controls` pointing to its panel ID (already done in faqRegistry parse), and verify `open` sets `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table column headers lack aria‑colindex or scope attributes for screen‑reader navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table uses `data-plan-col` attributes but does not provide `aria‑colindex` or `scope="col"` on header cells, making column relationships ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(th, 'scope', 'col')` and `setAttr(th, 'aria-colindex', String(index+1))` for each header cell.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not set aria‑describedby or aria‑label for its purpose
issuesFound.explanation: The drag‑and‑drop zone is a generic `<div>` without an accessible name, leaving AT users unaware of its function.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'role', 'button')` and `setAttr(_zoneEl, 'aria-label', _zoneEl.getAttribute('data-upload-label') || 'Upload files – click or drag files here')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid after correction
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation, the code removes `aria‑invalid` but does not explicitly set `aria‑invalid="false"`; some AT require the attribute to be present with a false value.
issuesFound.fix: In the `valid` branch, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Live‑region `#status-region` used for search status is hidden from AT when the panel is closed
issuesFound.explanation: The status element is inserted after the input but not given `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` until after the first interaction, and it may be hidden with `hidden` attribute, preventing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the status element always has `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` and is not hidden; instead, clear its text content when no message is needed.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation trigger button missing role and tabindex when not a <button> element
Dropdown menu panels lack role or aria‑label when not explicitly provided
File‑upload zone does not set aria‑describedby or aria‑label for its purpose

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function changes the visual state of the hamburger button and the navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(_open)); inside mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close** (or call setExpanded which already handles this).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button acts as a two‑state toggle (open/closed) but never updates aria‑pressed, violating the ARIA toggle pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (or open/close) add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open)); so the button reflects its pressed state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden with hidden attribute but no aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, mobileNav.close sets hidden on the drawer element but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so assistive technologies may still expose the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance adds/removes CSS classes and the hidden attribute on the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles the dropdown is a two‑state control but never updates aria‑pressed, violating the toggle button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In _onTriggerClick or after toggling, call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(inst.open));.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance sets hidden on the panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", leaving the panel in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); in _closeInstance and remove it in _openInstance.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open expands the answer panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, while close does set it to false. This inconsistency confuses assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In open add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hidden without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close sets hidden on the panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so the hidden content remains exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); in close and remove it in open.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields with error messages and CSS classes but never adds aria‑invalid="true", so screen‑reader users are not notified of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate after a failed validation call setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product filter hide/show uses only hidden attribute, no aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: filterAnimation.run adds hidden to elements being hidden but does not set aria‑hidden, leaving hidden products in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: When hiding an element, also call setAttr(el, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and remove it when showing.

issuesFound.title: Product filter UI updates (count badge, empty state) are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the product count badge and empty‑state message without any aria‑live announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware of result changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge or empty‑state, set textContent on an element with role="status" or aria‑live="polite" (e.g., reuse the existing liveRegion.announce call).

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips UI changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render adds/removes chip elements but does not provide an aria‑live announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of filter changes.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering chips, call liveRegion.announce with a summary like “Filters applied: …”.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks aria‑controls linking to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button does not have an aria‑controls attribute referencing the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after locating _drawer, ensure _drawer has an id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId) and set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger lacks aria‑controls linking to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria‑controls pointing to the associated panel, so assistive technologies cannot associate them.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register after creating inst, ensure inst.panel has an id and set setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-controls', inst.panel.id);.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger already has aria‑controls but missing aria‑expanded initial state
issuesFound.explanation: While aria‑controls is set, the trigger does not have an initial aria‑expanded value (should be “false” when collapsed), causing ambiguity.
issuesFound.fix: In faqRegistry.parse after setting aria-controls, also set setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); for collapsed items.

issuesFound.title: Site search suggestion list updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.render adds suggestion items but does not update an aria‑live region to announce the number of results, leaving users unaware of new suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call liveRegion.announce with a message like “5 suggestions available”.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing‑period toggle (month ↔ year) is a two‑state button but never updates aria‑pressed, violating the toggle button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing._onToggleClick or pricing.setPeriod, add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear));.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle reveals different price information but does not set aria‑expanded to indicate the expanded state of the price panel.
issuesFound.fix: When the period changes, set setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); or manage a region with aria‑expanded accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This breaks the utility's purpose, potentially exposing hidden content visually or hiding it from AT if combined with other rules.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element uses a transform transition for a 3D hover effect. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at line 848 is empty and does not disable this motion, which can cause vestibular disorders for users sensitive to movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 848: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements that rely on this selector, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px, but outline-offset does not create a visible ring if the outline width is 0. No box-shadow or border is provided as a replacement, hiding focus state from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers will announce the description but not the invalid state itself, confusing users about why the message appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2375: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Valid state does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` function removes the error description but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`. If a field was previously invalid, it remains marked as invalid in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2385: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's `aria-expanded` state is not updated to "false". Screen reader users will not know the menu is currently closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav overlay visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation overlay is shown/hidden using the `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is not updated to match. This can cause the overlay to remain in the accessibility tree when visually hidden or vice versa.

  Line 1313: _overlay.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded on open

issuesFound.fix: Line 1563: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function in dropdownMenu sets the hidden attribute on the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1576: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The register function in dropdownMenu sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1633: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The _open function in siteSearch sets the open state to true but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input. Screen reader users are not announced that the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2373: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The _close function in siteSearch sets the open state to false but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input. Screen reader users are not announced that the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2383: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The init function in siteSearch configures combobox roles but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the input. The initial closed state of the suggestion list is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2466: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in faqAccordion expands the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating a state mismatch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3557: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the product count badge when filters change, but does not announce this change via a live region. Users are unaware the result count has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2623: liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in pricing toggles the CSS checked class on the billing toggle switch but does not update the aria-checked attribute. The switch state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3152: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader does not update aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The show and hide functions in stickyHeader toggle CSS classes to visually hide the header but do not update aria-hidden. The header remains in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1779: _header.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); Line 1789: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4733: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function in srUtils removes the error description but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false". The field may remain marked as invalid to assistive technology after correction.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from visual users. It fails to position the content off-screen or clip it, meaning screen-reader-only text is displayed to all users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition and perspective rotation that persists even when prefers-reduced-motion is enabled because the media query block is empty and does not override the transition or transform properties.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use transform: translateY(-3px) on hover with a transition, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover, but the existing prefers-reduced-motion block only targets .pricing-card:hover, leaving the featured card animation active.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and auto dimensions, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 465: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)) but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images have a transform transition and perspective transform on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 814 is empty and does not disable the transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 814: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-slower); transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
