issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but these functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger elements. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1505: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of _openInstance
  Line 1520: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded via the open() function, the code adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetric state where expanded items are not properly announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the _renderResult function applies error styling and links the error message via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3210: In the invalid branch (else block), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or alongside the existing srUtils.markInvalid call

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference and clears visual error states, but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". This leaves the field marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` at the end of the markValid function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content. Keyboard users cannot immediately access the navigation links without tabbing through the rest of the page first.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1408: After `_lockScroll();`, add code to move focus to the drawer or first focusable element: `const firstLink = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstLink) firstLink.focus();` or `_drawer.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); _drawer.focus();`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned role="switch" but the _onToggleClick function only updates the visual state and internal state without updating the aria-checked attribute to reflect whether the switch is activated (annual) or not (monthly).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2605: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', next === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` inside _onToggleClick after determining the `next` value

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded to indicate when the suggestion listbox is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2080: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _open function
  Line 2090: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _close function

issuesFound.title: contactForm personal data inputs missing autocomplete attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for first name, last name, and email are selected via qs() but the code does not set autocomplete attributes on these inputs. Browsers cannot autofill these fields, and assistive technologies cannot identify their purpose programmatically.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2770: Add `contactFirstName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');`
  Line 2775: Add `contactLastName.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'family-name');`
  Line 2780: Add `contactEmail.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) indicate active state via CSS classes only. The function does not update aria-pressed to indicate which button is currently selected, failing to convey the toggle button state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2655: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after calculating isActive

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the module initializes. While the drawer starts closed, the missing attribute prevents assistive technologies from understanding the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1485: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer closes, it is visually hidden via CSS classes but remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users can still navigate to the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1425: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` inside close function
  Line 1408: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` inside open function to ensure it's accessible when visible

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content (the panel), but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or what content is now available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1510: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` or `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Menu opened');` inside _openInstance after the panel is shown

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer is a significant context change that reveals a large portion of the page content, but no announcement is made via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1408: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` inside the open function after the drawer is displayed

fpTitles: mobileNav.open missing focus management
contactForm personal data inputs missing autocomplete attributes

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the accessibility tree because the code only removes CSS classes without setting aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users can still navigate to the visually hidden drawer content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2910: In the `close` function, after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, keyboard focus is not returned to the toggle button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order and must tab backwards to find the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2910: In the `close` function, before the `emit` call, add `if (_trigger && document.contains(_trigger)) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when their panels are opened or closed. Screen reader users cannot perceive whether the dropdown panels are visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3010: In `_openInstance`, after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3025: In `_closeInstance`, after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed (including via Escape key), the `returnFocus` parameter is accepted but never used to move focus back to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their position in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3025: At the end of `_closeInstance`, add `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (though it is correctly set to "false" when closing). Screen reader users are not informed when content expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5600: In the `open` function, after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false". Assistive technologies assume the suggestion list is initially visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4090: In `init`, after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the inlineValidator marks fields visually with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen reader users are not informed that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6220: In `_renderResult`, inside the `else` block (invalid), after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the inlineValidator does not remove or set aria-invalid="false". Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6260: In `clearField`, after `srUtils.markValid(field, binding.errorEl);`, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the change in result count is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users do not receive feedback that filtering has occurred or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4480: In `applyFilters`, after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products match your filters`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price updates visually but the change is not announced. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have switched or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5000: In `setPeriod`, after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Billing switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed, the updated filter state is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users do not receive confirmation that a filter has been removed or that the filter set has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1980: At the end of the `render` function, add `if (chips.length) liveRegion.announce(`Active filters: ${chips.map(c => c.label).join(', ')}`);`

fpTitles: activeFilterChips changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open() and close() functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded or collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line open function: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line close function: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions show and hide the panel by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger) to reflect the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance function: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line _closeInstance function: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function before the setTimeout.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel is toggled open and closed, but the combobox input element (_inputEl) does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _open function: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line _close function: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button (_playBtn) cycles between two states (playing or paused) but does not set aria-pressed to indicate the active state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line togglePlay function: add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_playing));` after the ternary operation (or inside play() and pause() functions respectively).

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (_periodBtns) act as a single-select button group where one button is active, but the code only toggles CSS classes and does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncToggleState function inside forEach: add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid or valid, the code updates the aria-describedby relationship but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields, so screen readers cannot convey the validation state.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid function: add `setAttr(control, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(control, errorEl);`. Line markValid function: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` before removing aria-describedby (or replace with `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialised without setting aria-expanded="false" to indicate the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function after `_initialised = true;`: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are registered with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is never initialised to "false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line register function after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the suggestion panel is closed on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line init function after `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`: add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter hidden cards missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When product cards are filtered out and hidden via the hidden attribute, they are not removed from the accessibility tree with aria-hidden="true", so screen readers may still encounter them.
issuesFound.fix: Line run function inside toHide.forEach: add `setAttr(el, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` when setting the hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panels missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, it is hidden with the hidden attribute but aria-hidden="true" is not set, leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line _closeInstance function: add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` before or alongside `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panels missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ panel is closed, the hidden attribute is set after animation, but aria-hidden="true" is not set to remove it from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line close function inside onComplete callback: add `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` alongside `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox is hidden using the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden="true" is not set to ensure it is removed from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line hide function: add `setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` alongside `_listEl.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is hidden, it uses the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true" to ensure it is excluded from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line _update function: add `setAttr(_btn, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` alongside `_btn.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the product count badge is updated after filtering, the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region, so users are not informed of the result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line _updateCountBadge function: add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` after updating the textContent.

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When billing period prices are updated dynamically, the new prices are not announced to screen readers, so users may not be aware the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _updatePlanCard function after updating priceDisplay: add `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} per ${period}`);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter empty state not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter and the empty state message is shown, it is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users may not know why the grid is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line _updateEmptyState function inside if (count === 0) block: add `liveRegion.announce('No products match the selected filters');` after removing the hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed dynamically, the changes to the active filter list are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line render function after the chips loop (end of function if chips.length): add `liveRegion.announce(`Active filters: ${chips.map(c => c.label).join(', ')}`);`.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu panels missing aria-hidden when closed
faqAccordion panels missing aria-hidden when closed
scrollToTop button missing aria-hidden when hidden
activeFilterChips updates not announced

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button that controls the submenu has aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1034: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button updates its aria-label based on state but does not expose the expanded state via aria-expanded, leaving screen-reader users unaware when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button has aria-controls pointing to the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded, so assistive technology cannot announce whether the answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1684: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle uses IconButton without providing the required aria-label prop, resulting in a button with no accessible name. Screen-reader users hear only "button" with no indication of its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1184: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons indicate selection visually but do not expose the pressed state to assistive technology, making it impossible for screen-reader users to know which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1568: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}> and Line 1576: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is rendered as plain text without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" separately from the "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: Line 882: *

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state, the decorative spinner span is not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant noise alongside the loadingLabel text announced by VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 808: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation receives a visual modifier class but lacks aria-current="page", so screen-reader users cannot identify which page is active in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1056: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation is styled visually but not programmatically identified with aria-current, preventing screen-reader users from recognizing the active location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned list used for the seamless marquee animation is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1246: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is rendered as an article element without an accessible name, even though it contains a heading with an id that could be referenced to identify the card.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1600: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby, preventing it from being identified as a labelled region in landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section has a visually hidden heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not reference it with aria-labelledby, leaving the region unlabelled for screen-reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-links navigation landmark lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page (main nav, footer nav, etc.).
issuesFound.fix: Line 734: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation landmark does not have an accessible name, making it indistinguishable from other navigation regions when multiple nav elements are present.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1040: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Main">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile dialog lacks an aria-label, which is needed because the page contains multiple navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1095: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID ('newsletter-email-error') via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so the element with that ID is never rendered in the DOM. Screen readers will announce a reference to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1713: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}> and ensure ErrorMessage is rendered with that id.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input has aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-name-error', but FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop, so the error message element is not created in the DOM, breaking the accessibility relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1793: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the corresponding error message element is not rendered because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, leaving the reference dangling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1811: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input uses aria-describedby to reference 'contact-subject-error', but this ID does not exist in the DOM because FormGroup is not configured with the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1829: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references 'contact-message-error' via aria-describedby, but the error message element is never created because FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1847: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element has an onClick handler to close the navigation but is implemented as a div without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard event handlers, making it unreachable and unusable for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1088: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()} role="button" aria-label="Close navigation" tabIndex={0} />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay closes the dialog on click but is a non-interactive div element without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard handlers, preventing keyboard users from activating it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()} role="button" aria-label="Close dialog" tabIndex={0}>

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The dialog container has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which is required to indicate that the dialog is modal and that the rest of the page is inert while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1956: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay incorrect aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div incorrectly carries aria-modal="true". This attribute belongs on the dialog element itself, not on the backdrop/overlay, and its presence on the overlay can confuse assistive technology about which element is the actual modal container.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> (remove aria-modal="true" from this element)

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation is one of multiple nav elements on the page but lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from the mobile and footer navigations.
issuesFound.fix: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation body is a nav element but lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation landmarks on the page.
issuesFound.fix: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element with a heading inside, but lacks aria-labelledby to expose the plan name as the card's accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not set aria-busy on the button, so screen readers do not announce the loading state to users.
issuesFound.fix: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★/☆) are decorative content inside a container that already has an aria-label, but they are not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden, causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: {stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated integration list used for visual marquee looping is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) is decorative content inside a button that already has a text label, but it is not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden.
issuesFound.fix: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: Button spinner decorative icon not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span is decorative content (the button already has a VisuallyHidden loading label), but it lacks aria-hidden to prevent redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay aria-modal misplaced
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div, and the overlay has onClick without keyboard support, potentially confusing screen reader users about the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Remove aria-modal from overlay div and add to dialog div: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" separately from the "(required)" text that follows.
issuesFound.fix: *

fpTitles: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
MobileNav body landmark missing accessible name
PlanCard article missing accessible name
